about.title: About ADWA
about.subtitle: Empowering professional drivers with verified identity and welfare support since 2010
about.aboutText: All Drivers Welfare Association (ADWA) is India's leading digital platform dedicated to the welfare, registration, and empowerment of professional drivers. Founded in 2010, we have grown to serve over 12 lakh registered drivers across 28 states.
about.visionText: A future where every professional driver in India has verified digital identity, comprehensive welfare coverage, and a strong collective voice that is heard at every level of government.
about.missionText: To provide professional drivers with digital identity credentials, welfare benefits, and a unified support network that upholds their dignity, security, and professional recognition across India.
about.objectivesSubtitle: What we stand for and work towards every day
about.whoWeAreDesc1: All Drivers Welfare Association (ADWA) is India's leading digital platform dedicated to the welfare, registration, and empowerment of professional drivers. Founded in 2010, we have grown to serve over 12 lakh registered drivers across 28 states.
about.whoWeAreDesc2: Our mission is to provide every professional driver with a verified digital identity, comprehensive welfare coverage, and a strong support network that upholds their dignity and security.
about.welfareProgramsSubtitle: Benefits available to all active ADWA members
about.leadershipSubtitle: Experienced leaders dedicated to driver welfare
objectives: Provide digital identity credentials to all professional drivers.
Create a national database of verified drivers for transport companies.
Offer accident insurance, medical assistance, and emergency support.
Advocate for drivers' rights and welfare at state and national levels.
Simplify renewal and registration through digital platforms.
Bridge the gap between drivers and government welfare schemes.

welfare.title: Accident Insurance
welfare.desc: Coverage support for road accident victims among our members.

welfare.title: Medical Assistance
welfare.desc: Help with hospital bills and medical expenses in emergencies.

welfare.title: Legal Aid
welfare.desc: Free legal consultation for members facing traffic disputes.

welfare.title: Education Support
welfare.desc: Scholarship assistance for children of active members.

welfare.title: Financial Aid
welfare.desc: Hardship grants for members in genuine financial distress.

welfare.title: Family Welfare
welfare.desc: Support for families of deceased or permanently disabled members.

timeline.event: ADWA founded in New Delhi with 200 founding members.
Expanded to 10 states. Launched first membership ID card program.
Registered 1 lakh members. Launched welfare fund.
Digital ID card system launched. Mobile-friendly portal released.
Launched full digital platform. 12+ lakh members, 28 states.

rules.title: Rules & Guidelines
rules.subtitle: Important guidelines for ADWA members and applicants
safety: Always follow traffic signals, lane discipline, speed limits and road safety rules.
Carry all valid documents: driving license, vehicle registration, insurance, and PUC.
Never drive under the influence of alcohol, drugs, or any substance that impairs judgment.
Avoid use of mobile phones or any distracting device while operating the vehicle.
Wear seat belts at all times and ensure all passengers are buckled up.
Respect pedestrians and give way at zebra crossings and school zones.
Maintain vehicle in good condition: check brakes, tyres, lights, and mirrors regularly.
Report accidents and emergencies immediately to traffic police and ADWA helpline.
membership: Membership is open to all professional drivers with a valid driving license and Aadhaar card.
Each driver may hold only one active ADWA membership at any time.
Membership is non-transferable and cannot be shared with other individuals.
Members must keep their contact information (mobile, email, address) up to date.
Membership fee must be paid in full before the card is issued.
ADWA reserves the right to cancel membership for violation of code of conduct.
Members must report any change in driving license status to ADWA within 30 days.
renewal: Membership must be renewed before or within 90 days of expiry to avoid re-registration.
After 90 days of expiry, a fresh registration process must be initiated.
The renewal fee is ₹250 (subject to revision as announced by ADWA).
All renewed memberships are valid for 1 year from the date of renewal approval.
Renewal applications are processed within 7-10 working days.
Members can apply for renewal online through the ADWA portal.
Physical renewal through district offices is also available.
privacy: ADWA collects personal information solely for the purpose of driver registration, membership management, and welfare services.
Your Aadhaar number is stored in encrypted form and is never shared with any third party without your explicit consent.
Profile photos and documents uploaded are stored securely and used only for ID card generation and verification purposes.
We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal information to any commercial entity.
Members may request deletion of their data by contacting our support team. However, certain records may be retained as required by law.
Our platform uses industry-standard SSL/TLS encryption for all data transmission.
